original_hypothesis.title: BOLDUpgradeAction Caps Staker Refund at 50 Leaving Remaining Stakers Dependent on Patched Logic
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: BOLDUpgradeAction.cleanupOldRollup iterates stakers with a hard cap of 50. Stakers at index 50+ are not refunded during the upgrade. They must manually withdraw via the IMPL_PATCHED_OLD_ROLLUP_USER implementation. Additionally, stakers currently in a challenge (currentChallenge != 0) are skipped even within the first 50. If IMPL_PATCHED_OLD_ROLLUP_USER doesn't properly handle all edge cases (challenged stakers, specific stake states), those stakers' funds could be permanently locked in the old rollup contract.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The 50-staker cap is an arbitrary limit that could leave funds inaccessible if the patched implementation has any gaps in handling remaining stakers' withdrawal cases.
deployment_assumptions: Rollup has more than 50 stakers at upgrade time
IMPL_PATCHED_OLD_ROLLUP_USER correctly handles all staker withdrawal cases
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: BOLDUpgradeAction.sol cleanupOldRollup at lines within perform function, cap at stakerCount > 50

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (stakerCount > 50) { stakerCount = 50; } - hard cap on staker iteration; stakers with currentChallenge != 0 also skipped

preconditions: Old rollup has more than 50 active stakers
Some stakers beyond index 50 have locked funds

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Hard cap of 50 in for-loop; remaining stakers depend entirely on IMPL_PATCHED_OLD_ROLLUP_USER correctness

operational_questions: How many stakers do production Arbitrum rollups typically have?
Does IMPL_PATCHED_OLD_ROLLUP_USER handle all staker states correctly?
Can challenged stakers withdraw after the old rollup is paused?
validation_requirements: Test withdrawal by staker at index 51+ after upgrade
Test withdrawal by challenged staker after upgrade

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Before upgrade, create many staker positions (>50) to exceed the cap
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Upgrade executes, only first 50 eligible stakers are refunded
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Remaining stakers must manually interact with paused old rollup
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: If patched implementation has bugs, funds may be permanently locked

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Stakers' funds temporarily or permanently locked in old rollup
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Depends on IMPL_PATCHED_OLD_ROLLUP_USER correctness; may need another upgrade

execution_requirements_analysis.coordination_complexity: High - requires many staker positions before known upgrade

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Staker count exceeding 50 before upgrade proposal
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Stakers unable to withdraw after upgrade
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Sudden increase in staker count before upgrade

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
